data.prompt:
<execute-bead>
  <instructions>
You are executing one bead inside an isolated DDx execution worktree. The bead's <description> and <acceptance> are the completion contract — every AC must be provably satisfied by a specific code, test, or file you can point to after your commit.

## Step 0: size check

The bead is too big when any of these holds:

- More than ~6 ACs spanning unrelated subsystems.
- AC mixes design, implementation, integration tests, and docs as separate top-level deliverables.
- Description names multiple feature-sized sub-pieces.
- More than ~500 lines across more than ~5 files in unrelated packages.
- If the bead description exceeds 8000 bytes, treat Step 0 as a split-first pass and favor child-bead scoping before implementation.
- Auto-decomposition is capped at depth 2: root beads may split once, decomposed children may split once more, and third-level splits must be rejected with an explanation.

If too big, decompose — do not attempt the work:

1. `ddx bead create` for each child slice (focused title, description, AC; copy parent's labels and spec-id).
2. `ddx bead dep add <child-id> <parent-id>` to record edges.
3. `ddx bead update <parent-id> --notes 'decomposed into <child-ids>'` so the decomposition is visible.
4. Write `.ddx/executions/20260506T100540-6ca9c889/no_changes_rationale.txt` listing each child ID and slice, then stop.

A clean decomposition is a successful attempt. Do not mix partial implementation with decomposition.

## How to work

- Read first. If the bead names files, specs, or prior beads, read them before editing — do not guess.
- Cross-reference each AC against concrete evidence (test name, file path, function) before committing. If you cannot point at it, it is not done.
- Run the project's test and lint commands before committing. **Do not commit red code** — fix failures first.
- Stage with `git add <specific-paths>`; never `git add -A` (the worktree may have unrelated WIP).
- Commit exactly once when green; conventional-commit subject ending with `[<bead-id>]`. Stop after the commit.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's scope.
- If you cannot finish, write `.ddx/executions/20260506T100540-6ca9c889/no_changes_rationale.txt` (what is done, what blocks, what a follow-up needs) before exiting. No commit and no rationale ⇒ DDx records `no_evidence_produced`. A well-justified no_changes beats a bad commit.

## no_changes contract

If you write `.ddx/executions/20260506T100540-6ca9c889/no_changes_rationale.txt`, it MUST carry one of:

- `verification_command: <one-line shell command>` — the loop runs it (cwd = project root, 60s); exit 0 closes the bead, non-zero rejects.
- `status: needs_investigation` + `reason: <why>` — bead stays open for triage.

Bare rationales ("nothing to do") are rejected.

## Reports

Freestanding artifacts (investigation reports, findings docs) go under `.ddx/executions/20260506T100540-6ca9c889/` (the per-attempt evidence directory under `.ddx/executions/`). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository** — out-of-repo paths are invisible to the post-merge reviewer and cause BLOCK on missing evidence. If the bead names a specific in-repo path, use it; else default to `.ddx/executions/20260506T100540-6ca9c889/<short-name>.md`. Stage and commit the report alongside code.

## Bead overrides project defaults

The bead description and AC override CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and conservative project defaults (YAGNI, DOWITYTD, no-docs-unless-asked) in this worktree — write whatever the bead asks for.

## Review gate

- The review is a gate, not an escape hatch — meet every AC in this pass.
- Address every BLOCKING `<review-findings>` item; do not declare `no_changes` with blocking findings open.

## Constraints

- Work only inside this execution worktree.
- Keep `.ddx/executions/` intact — DDx uses it as execution evidence.
- **Never run `ddx init`** — the workspace is initialized; running it corrupts the bead queue.
- Do not modify files outside the bead's named scope.
- Do not rewrite CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, or other project-instructions files unless the bead asks.

## When the work is done

After the commit succeeds and every AC is verified, stop. Return control to the orchestrator — do not keep exploring or testing.
  </instructions>
  <bead id="ddx-317d0254">
    <title>Add default prose rules and DDx vocabulary</title>
    <description>
CONTEXT
DDx needs deterministic default prose rules and vocabulary so the CLI can flag common AI-writing tropes without relying on subjective model judgment. The rules must be tuned for DDx technical documents and configurable by projects.

IN-SCOPE FILES
- Default prose rule/vocabulary assets under the path selected by TD-036
- Config schema/defaults for prose settings if TD-036 places them in this slice
- cli/internal/docprose tests that evaluate rules against the fixtures from the fixture bead

RULE REQUIREMENTS
- Detect unsupported generic praise and vague claims such as robust, seamless, comprehensive, industry-leading-style phrases when not backed by specifics.
- Detect repeated formulaic openings or filler transitions where practical.
- Support project vocabulary accept/reject lists.
- Support at least technical and planning modes; public mode may share defaults if TD-036 says so.
- Keep findings advisory metadata-ready: rule id, severity, rationale, suggested edit.

OUT-OF-SCOPE
- ddx doc prose command wiring.
- Review integration.
- Any AI-authorship score.
    </description>
    <acceptance>
1. Default rules/vocabulary are present in the asset path chosen by TD-036 and include DDx terminology accepts plus unsupported filler rejects.
2. Focused tests run the rules against the fixture/golden cases and prove expected positives and negatives.
3. Project vocabulary override behavior is covered by a test using accept and reject entries.
4. No output includes an AI-authorship score or detector-bypass claim.
5. `cd cli && go test ./internal/docprose/...` passes.
6. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
    </acceptance>
    <labels>phase:2, area:cli, area:plugins, kind:feature, prose-quality</labels>
    <metadata parent="ddx-ccda7a32" spec-id="TD-036" base-rev="aacbfdc7008bdf4be18a6c854819a7b2d6409ea9" bundle=".ddx/executions/20260506T100540-6ca9c889"/>
  </bead>
  <governing>
    <ref id="TD-036" path="docs/helix/02-design/technical-designs/TD-036-prose-quality-pipeline.md">Technical Design: Prose Quality Pipeline</ref>
  </governing>
</execute-bead>

data.output:
Implemented and committed as `79fb3aca7` (`feat(docprose): add default prose rules and vocabulary [ddx-317d0254]`).

What changed:
- Added the deterministic docprose checker and asset loaders in [cli/internal/docprose/checker.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-317d0254-20260506T100540-6ca9c889/cli/internal/docprose/checker.go#L24) and [cli/internal/docprose/assets.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-317d0254-20260506T100540-6ca9c889/cli/internal/docprose/assets.go#L12).
- Wired fixture-driven tests to execute the checker against the existing golden corpus in [cli/internal/docprose/fixtures_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-317d0254-20260506T100540-6ca9c889/cli/internal/docprose/fixtures_test.go#L84).
- Added focused coverage for schema shape, changed/path mode behavior, and vocabulary overrides in [cli/internal/docprose/docprose_test.go](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-317d0254-20260506T100540-6ca9c889/cli/internal/docprose/docprose_test.go#L10).
- Added the default prose config, DDx accept vocabulary, and mode-specific rule packs under [library/checks/prose-quality/check.yaml](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-317d0254-20260506T100540-6ca9c889/library/checks/prose-quality/check.yaml#L1), [library/checks/prose-quality/vocabulary/default.yaml](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-317d0254-20260506T100540-6ca9c889/library/checks/prose-quality/vocabulary/default.yaml#L1), [library/checks/prose-quality/rules/technical.yaml](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-317d0254-20260506T100540-6ca9c889/library/checks/prose-quality/rules/technical.yaml#L1), [planning.yaml](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-317d0254-20260506T100540-6ca9c889/library/checks/prose-quality/rules/planning.yaml#L1), and [public.yaml](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-317d0254-20260506T100540-6ca9c889/library/checks/prose-quality/rules/public.yaml#L1).

Verification:
- `cd cli && go test ./internal/docprose/...`
- `lefthook run pre-commit`

One note: the repo hook’s `go test -race` step required `CGO_ENABLED=1`, so the final commit was created with cgo enabled to satisfy the hook.
